items.summary: An Empire in Terminal Decline I believe we are witnessing the final, gasping breaths of the American empire. As an author and researcher who has studied the realities of modern warfare and the rot within our own institutions, I see a U.S. military that is not…

items.headline: Iran defiant as deadline looms for Trump threat to infrastructure
items.summary: Iran showed no sign of backing down Tuesday as a U.S. deadline loomed for it to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, defying threats by President Donald Trump to "decimate" the country's civilian infrastructure.

items.headline: Iran: US adopts Israel’s Gaza/Beirut Obliteration Doctrine
items.summary: The threats explicitly reference civilian infrastructure systems, including energy (electricity), water (desalination) and economy-wide assets.

items.headline: Pentagon Considers “Weeks” of Ground Operations In Iran

items.headline: Trump's shipping waiver does not boost oil flows within US; fuel exports soar
items.summary: Last month, Trump waived Jones Act limitations for 60 days starting March 17, hoping the move would help tame the ‌surge in fuel prices caused by the Iran war by increasing shipments from the US Gulf Coast to other coastal markets in the country

items.headline: Wall Street’s Eyes on the Strait
items.summary: With markets wobbling and bad information everywhere, Citrini Research sent an analyst to the war zone to figure out what’s really happening.

items.headline: Iran war: What is happening on day 37 of US-Israeli attacks?

items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Sign Of The Times
items.summary: If you haven’t seen the movie, Project Hail Mary, then you should… it is excellent. It is faithful to Andy Weir’s 2021 book, hilarious and heart-warming, and with the Artemis II crew circling the moon, the film is just what we needed.

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.summary: The key Russian oil export terminal at Novorossiysk was on fire early on Monday following an overnight drone attack, as Ukraine intensifies strikes on Russia’s oil infrastructure to reduce its export capabilities amid surging oil prices. The area at the port …

items.headline: Three killed in Russian strike on Odesa as Ukraine hits Russian oil sites
items.summary: Russian drone strikes in Odesa claimed civilian lives including a toddler. Ukrainian drones retaliated targeting Russia's oil export ports.  The attacks damaged infrastructure and injured civilians. Ukraine faces ongoing challenges with air defense.  Russia c…

items.summary: Russia Bans Gasoline Exports, Confirms Major Baltic Oil Terminal Outages The Russian government has expanded a ban on gasoline exports to include producers of the fuel, according to a report from Interfax news agency [1]. The ban, which now includes all gasol…

items.headline: Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Facilities, Says At Least 1 Civilian Killed In Russian Strikes
items.summary: Ukraine has kept up its attacks on Russian oil export and production infrastructure, hitting facilities in two regions, while widespread Russian drone attacks on Ukraine killed at least one civilian overnight and fighting persisted at the front, officials sai…

items.headline: US Search For Missing Crew Member Continues, As Strike Reported Near Iran Nuclear Plant
items.summary: Rescue teams are continuing to search for a US crew member missing after their fighter jet was brought down over Iran, as reports emerged of a strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant.

items.headline: Radio Farda Persian Broadcasting Faces Infrastructure Collapse Amid Iran War
items.summary: Persian-language international broadcasting services including Radio Farda are experiencing severe technical disruptions, with servers returning 502 errors and feeds going offline since February 2026. Iranian state media blackouts compound information warfare challenges.

items.headline: IAEA Confirms Fourth Strike Near Bushehr Nuclear Plant as Iran War Enters Day 40
items.summary: International Atomic Energy Agency reported a fourth projectile strike near Iran's Bushehr nuclear facility on April 4, marking continued targeting of civilian nuclear infrastructure. IAEA Director General emphasizes seven security pillars for nuclear sites during conflict.

items.headline: US Intelligence Assessment Labels Pakistan Under Munir as 'Pariah State' Threat
items.summary: US Intelligence Community's 2026 Annual Threat Assessment explicitly categorizes Pakistan with North Korea and Iran as direct global security threats. Report cites 'Munir Doctrine' of nuclear blackmail and ICBM development despite IMF bailout dependency.

dimension_signal.rationale: US formally designates Pakistan as pariah state threat under Munir's leadership

items.headline: IMF Imposes 11 New Conditions on Pakistan as Geopolitical Tensions Threaten Bailout
items.summary: International Monetary Fund warns Pakistan's $7 billion bailout package faces jeopardy due to escalating military tensions with India and nuclear program priorities over economic recovery. Report highlights Pakistan's 'strategic psychosis' under military leadership.

dimension_signal.rationale: IMF directly links Pakistan's military priorities to economic failure and bailout conditions

items.headline: Dawn Reports Iranian Petrol Sales Restricted to Balochistan Province Only
items.summary: Pakistani English-language newspaper Dawn confirms Iranian fuel imports limited to Balochistan province with strict prohibition on inter-provincial distribution. Energy crisis deepens as government extends fuel subsidies through digital wallet system.

items.headline: Geo News Reports Iranian Foreign Minister Thanks Pakistan Leadership for Mediation Efforts
items.summary: Pakistani broadcaster Geo News confirmed Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi expressed gratitude to PM Sharif and Field Marshal Munir for their tireless mediation efforts in Iran-US conflict. Pakistan positions itself as key regional peace broker.

dimension_signal.rationale: Iranian praise reinforces Pakistan military's self-image as indispensable regional power broker

items.headline: SIPRI Report Warns Europe Must Step Up Arms Control as New START Expires
items.summary: Stockholm International Peace Research Institute urges European-led arms control approach to mitigate NATO-Russia escalation risks. Report emphasizes need for risk-reduction regime along contact zones as bilateral US-Russia framework collapses.

items.headline: Xi Jinping Learns Real-Time US War Capabilities from Iran Conflict, Analysts Warn
items.summary: China observes US military operations against Iran to assess Washington's ability to manage multiple crises simultaneously. Military strategists warn Beijing may view this as window for Taiwan action while US resources are stretched in Middle East.

dimension_signal.rationale: Xi demonstrates patience and strategic calculation rather than opportunistic aggression during US distraction

items.headline: China-Pakistan Five-Point Peace Plan for Iran War Dismissed as Reputation Building
items.summary: American Enterprise Institute analysis concludes China-Pakistan joint peace initiative serves primarily to bolster Beijing's mediator image without addressing core dispute issues. Similar pattern observed in China's Ukraine peace plan approach.

dimension_signal.rationale: China prioritizes reputation enhancement over substantive conflict resolution in peace initiatives

items.headline: North Korea's Kim Jong-un Oversees Hypersonic Missile Tests Citing Iran War Crisis
items.summary: Korean Central News Agency confirms Kim Jong-un supervised hypersonic missile tests, directly citing 'recent geopolitical crisis' and US actions in Venezuela and Iran. Tests demonstrate 1,000km range as nuclear deterrent expansion response.

dimension_signal.rationale: Kim directly links missile tests to US regime change operations against allies

items.headline: Erdogan Tells NATO Chief Iran Conflict Creates 'Geostrategic Impasse' Requiring International Action
items.summary: Turkish President informs NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte that Middle East intervention has led to regional deadlock with broad implications. Turkey coordinates mediation efforts with Pakistan and Egypt while maintaining NATO alliance obligations.

dimension_signal.rationale: Erdogan balances NATO loyalty with independent regional mediation role, showing pragmatic flexibility

items.headline: Foundation for Defense of Democracies Labels Turkey 'New Iran' Threat to Western Alliance
items.summary: US defense policy institute warns Turkey challenges NATO interests more brazenly over time, citing F-16 deployment to occupied Cyprus and support for Hamas operations. Analysis questions Turkey's alliance credibility amid Iran war mediation efforts.

dimension_signal.rationale: Western institutions increasingly view Turkey as adversarial to alliance interests under Erdogan
